title: Availability, Disruption Budgets, Graceful Draining, Autoscaling, and Capacity · Control-Plane Trace: K8s Replica Failure Domain Capacity
summary: A two-zone service scales from noisy metrics, violates its recovery target during drain, and has enough replicas but insufficient failure-domain and resource headroom. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s replica failure domain capacity increment in availability and autoscaling operating policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Translate service objectives into replica count, failure domains, topology placement, readiness, surge capacity, dependency limits, and recovery evidence.
Separate scheduling priority, preemption, nomination, PodDisruptionBudget interaction, node-pressure eviction, API eviction, graceful termination, and fairness.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Predict K8s Replica Failure Domain Capacity · Theory K8s Replica Failure Domain Capacity
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory service has 6 replicas but all share one zone, a strict disruption budget blocks drain, and HPA oscillates because requests and metrics are wrong. Balance failure domains, PDB, rollout surge, autoscaling signals, stabilization, and capacity.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the K8s Replica Failure Domain Capacity contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example service has 6 replicas but all share one zone, a strict disruption budget blocks drain, and HPA oscillates because requests and metrics are wrong. Balance failure domains, PDB, rollout surge, autoscaling signals, stabilization, and capacity. Competency probe: Translate service objectives into replica count, failure domains, topology placement, readiness, surge capacity, dependency limits, and recovery evidence. A defensible solution applies K8s Replica Failure Domain Capacity, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to availability and autoscaling operating policy.

content.code:
# Worked focus: k8s-replica-failure-domain-capacity — Translate service objectives into replica count, failure domains, topology placement, readiness, surge capacity, dependency limits, and recovery evidence.
  # Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-availability-disruption-autoscaling-2
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-availability-disruption-autoscaling-2
    artifact: availability-policy.yaml
    decision: worked k8s availability disruption autoscaling 2
    environment: rootless development cluster with an empty namespace
    fault-injection: make an admission dependency unavailable during an upgrade
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      objective: 99.9-percent-availability
      replicas: 4-across-2-zones
      pdb: minAvailable-3
      hpa: autoscaling-v2-cpu-and-queue
      stabilization: bounded
      node-headroom: measured
      faults: drain-zone-loss-metrics-gap
    verify: kubectl get deployment,pdb,hpa -n intake -o json
    failure: Reject replica-count-only HA, PDB as involuntary-failure protection, limit-based HPA math, or autoscalers without capacity and feedback analysis.
    repair: Tie replicas and topology to objectives, calculate disruption and scaling, reserve headroom, inject lag and failure, and measure user-visible service.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Three replicas always provide high availability regardless of nodes, zones, storage, or shared dependencies. This fails the K8s Replica Failure Domain Capacity contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
